notes: Run these in a COPY of the repo (never the real one). With-skill = point the agent at .claude/skills/run-skill-generator/SKILL.md; baseline = same copy with .claude/ and CLAUDE.md removed (and skill mentions filtered from state files). Grade the resulting working tree against the expectations.

evals.name: bootstrap-session
evals.prompt: Start the next work session on CuffBot in this repository. No specific request from the owner this time — do whatever the project needs next, and finish the session properly.
evals.expected_output: Agent orients on STATE.md (resume point), builds the next roadmap milestone per references/architecture.md, adds passing node:test tests, writes the module manual from the template, updates STATE.md with a new resume point, appends a SESSION_LOG.md entry, and records a self-improvement outcome. All artifacts in English; no secrets committed.
expectations: package.json exists, parses as JSON, and declares discord.js as a dependency
Every .js file under src/ passes node --check (syntax-clean)
Modular layout respected: at least one module directory under src/modules/ exports a manifest via index.js
At least one slash command is defined (SlashCommandBuilder with setName somewhere under src/)
A module manual exists under docs/modules/ containing the template sections: Purpose, Commands, Configuration, Permissions & safety, How it works, Testing, Troubleshooting
npm test runs and exits 0
STATE.md was updated by the session: Last updated advanced beyond the previous session and a non-empty resume point exists for the next session
SESSION_LOG.md gained a new appended session entry without rewriting earlier entries
A self-improvement trail was recorded: skill CHANGELOG/LEARNINGS entry, or an explicit no-change-because note in the session log
No secrets committed: no tracked .env, no token-like literals, .env.example holds placeholders only
All produced artifacts (code, docs, state files, commit messages) are in English
The session's work was committed (new commit(s) on top of the starting HEAD)

evals.name: continuation-detects-drift
evals.expected_output: The seeded repo's STATE.md/SESSION_LOG.md claim more than is true (broken syntax claimed clean, a command and manual claimed done but missing, failing tests claimed green). Agent runs the verification block, detects the drift, corrects STATE.md, records the corrections in a new SESSION_LOG.md entry, repairs or honestly re-scopes the broken work BEFORE building anything new, and leaves the repo consistent.
files: Seeded drift fixture — a previous session that over-reported its progress. Seed recipe: introduce a syntax error in one core file, remove one claimed command file and the claimed manual, keep the log/STATE claiming all of it is done and green.
expectations: Drift was detected: the new session log entry (Corrections) or corrected STATE.md explicitly names at least two of the seeded discrepancies
The false claims were not silently trusted: the new log entry's Corrections section is present and not 'None'
STATE.md was corrected: after the session, every claim in it is actually true in the working tree
The broken code was repaired before new feature work: node --check passes on every src/ file at session end
npm test runs and exits 0 at session end
The claimed-but-missing manual now exists following the template, or its absence is explicitly recorded as an open problem in STATE.md
SESSION_LOG.md gained a new appended entry; earlier entries remain unmodified
Fixture git history retained (seeded commits still ancestors; work committed on top)
No secrets committed: no tracked .env, no token-like literals, .env.example holds placeholders only
All produced artifacts are in English
